User Story: As a student, I want the platform to track my knowledge state in real-time so that I receive content at my appropriate difficulty level
Acceptance Criteria: System implements BKT or DKT with AUC >= 0.80 for performance prediction; updates knowledge state after each interaction; adjusts next item difficulty within 200ms
Notes/Evidence Base: Evidence: Mai et al. (2025) - Transformer-Bayesian hybrid achieves AUC 0.847

User Story: As a student, I want to review concepts at optimal intervals so that I retain knowledge long-term
Acceptance Criteria: Spaced repetition engine schedules review based on forgetting curve; implements SM2 or LSTM-based algorithm; interval adjusts based on performance
Notes/Evidence Base: Evidence: Cao & Carvalho (2025) - adaptive spaced retrieval improves accuracy in Algebra I

User Story: As a student, I must demonstrate mastery before advancing so that I don't have gaps in prerequisite knowledge
Acceptance Criteria: Mastery threshold configurable (default 80%); remediation loop provides alternative explanations and worked examples when threshold not met; progress blocked until mastery achieved
Notes/Evidence Base: Evidence: Sutiawan et al. (2025) - mastery learning with adaptive quizzes shows Cohen's d=1.2

User Story: As a neurodiverse student, I need full keyboard navigation and screen reader support so I can access all content
Acceptance Criteria: 100% of interactive elements keyboard accessible (Tab/Shift+Tab); focus indicators visible; ARIA labels present; tested with NVDA/JAWS/VoiceOver; passes WCAG 2.1 Level AA
Notes/Evidence Base: COPPA/FERPA compliance requires accessibility; WCAG 2.1 Level AA mandatory by April 2026-2027

User Story: As a student with reading difficulties, I want text-to-speech on all content so I can access grade-level material
Acceptance Criteria: TTS available on all text content; speed adjustable (0.5x-2x); highlight word-by-word; supports MathML for equation reading; works with platform navigation
Notes/Evidence Base: Critical for 504/IEP compliance; evidence shows TTS reduces cognitive load for decoding

User Story: As an ELL student, I want cognate highlighting and visual scaffolding so I can access content while learning English
Acceptance Criteria: System identifies cognates (e.g., fracción/fraction) and highlights them; provides picture glossary for academic vocabulary; reduces linguistic load on math items
Notes/Evidence Base: Gap identified in competitive analysis—no platform offers cognate support

User Story: As a teacher, I need an early warning system to identify at-risk students so I can intervene before unit tests
Acceptance Criteria: Dashboard shows at-risk flag when BKT/DKT predicts <50% success on upcoming material; updates daily; filterable by class/section; one-click view of specific skill gaps
Notes/Evidence Base: Evidence: DKT/BKT F1-scores ~0.76 for early at-risk identification

User Story: As a teacher, I want to assign specific standards and see mastery by standard so I can align to my pacing guide
Acceptance Criteria: Assignment creation supports CCSS/NGSS standard selection; progress view shows % mastery by standard and student; exports to gradebook (CSV/OneRoster)
Notes/Evidence Base: Required for classroom integration per competitive analysis

User Story: As a parent, I need verifiable parental consent workflow for my under-13 child so I comply with COPPA
Acceptance Criteria: Age-gating triggers VPC workflow for users <13; supports electronic consent form, credit card verification, or school-mediated consent; documents consent with timestamp; parental rights portal for access/deletion requests
Notes/Evidence Base: COPPA 15 U.S.C. §6501-6506 requires VPC for under-13 users

User Story: As a district administrator, I need FERPA-compliant Data Protection Agreements so I can legally deploy the platform
Acceptance Criteria: DPA template provided; specifies data elements, use restrictions, security measures, subcontractor provisions, destruction timeline; school maintains direct control; no re-disclosure without authorization
Notes/Evidence Base: FERPA §99.31(a)(1)(i) school official exception requires written contract

User Story: As a teacher, I want to author or modify adaptive content for my specific students so I can differentiate instruction
Acceptance Criteria: Content authoring tool allows creation of adaptive items with metadata (difficulty, standards, prerequisites); supports multiple question types; can modify existing content for specific students; shares within district
Notes/Evidence Base: Major gap identified in competitive analysis—no platform offers teacher authoring

User Story: As a student with math anxiety, I want to see my growth when working below grade level so I maintain motivation
Acceptance Criteria: Progress view shows growth metrics (concepts mastered, time spent, accuracy trends) not just grade level; celebrates effort and strategy use; no public comparison to peers
Notes/Evidence Base: Growth mindset research (Dweck)—avoid ability labeling

User Story: As a high-achieving student, I want to explore beyond the standard curriculum so I stay engaged
Acceptance Criteria: System identifies mastery ceiling and offers enrichment content; supports exploration mode for tangential topics; provides authentic problem-solving challenges
Notes/Evidence Base: Evidence: Agrawal et al. (2022)—heavy users with niche preferences benefit most from personalization

User Story: As a district administrator, I need district-wide learning analytics so I can identify equity gaps
Acceptance Criteria: District dashboard aggregates across schools; filters by demographic subgroups; identifies usage gaps and outcome disparities; exports ESSA evidence documentation
Notes/Evidence Base: Required for ESSA Tier II/III evidence generation

User Story: As a parent, I want weekly progress summaries in my language so I can support my child
Acceptance Criteria: Automated weekly email with progress summary; available in Spanish and other languages; includes specific suggestions for home support; links to activity details
Notes/Evidence Base: COPPA requires transparency; parent engagement correlates with outcomes

User Story: As a special education teacher, I need progress monitoring aligned to IEP goals so I can document services
Acceptance Criteria: IEP goal import/mapping; progress tracks against IEP objectives; generates standards-based IEP progress reports; exports to IEP systems (PowerSchool, SEDS)
Notes/Evidence Base: Required for SPED compliance; major gap in existing platforms

User Story: As a user, I expect page load times under 200ms so the experience feels responsive
Acceptance Criteria: 95th percentile API response time <200ms; supports 1000+ concurrent users per instance; implements CDN for static assets; progressive loading for complex content
Notes/Evidence Base: Cognitive load theory—delays increase extraneous load

User Story: As a district, I need interoperability with existing tools so implementation is seamless
Acceptance Criteria: Supports LTI 1.3 Advantage for LMS integration; Clever rostering and SSO; Google Classroom integration; OneRoster gradebook export; xAPI for learning analytics
Notes/Evidence Base: Universal expectation per competitive analysis—table stakes

User Story: As an ELL student, I want to take diagnostics in my native language so my math ability is accurately assessed
Acceptance Criteria: Math diagnostic available in Spanish and other languages; results inform English-language instruction with appropriate scaffolding; L1-L2 conceptual mapping
Notes/Evidence Base: Would improve diagnostic validity but adds significant content cost

User Story: As a student, I want an AI tutor that I can chat with so I can ask questions
Acceptance Criteria: Conversational AI tutor (like Khanmigo) integrated; natural language question answering; Socratic questioning approach
Notes/Evidence Base: Evidence base still emerging (Agrawal et al. 2022 shows benefits but implementation varies); defer to Phase 2 due to safety/compliance complexity

User Story: System shall maintain 99.9% uptime during school hours (7am-5pm local time) so learning is not disrupted
Acceptance Criteria: Measured monthly; scheduled maintenance outside school hours; automatic failover; status page for incident communication
Notes/Evidence Base: Critical for school operations—teachers depend on platform for instruction

User Story: System shall encrypt all student data at rest and in transit so privacy is protected
Acceptance Criteria: AES-256 encryption at rest; TLS 1.3 in transit; key rotation policy; no plaintext storage of PII; audit logging of data access
Notes/Evidence Base: COPPA/FERPA data security requirements; SOPIPA and state laws

User Story: System shall conform to WCAG 2.1 Level AA so all students can access content
Acceptance Criteria: Passes automated accessibility testing (axe, Lighthouse); manual testing with screen readers; keyboard navigation validation; color contrast verification
Notes/Evidence Base: DOJ final rule requires compliance by April 2026-2027

User Story: System shall support 1,000,000 concurrent users with <10% performance degradation so it scales to large districts
Acceptance Criteria: Load tested to 1M concurrent; horizontal scaling architecture; database sharding strategy; CDN for global content delivery
Notes/Evidence Base: Required for large district contracts

User Story: System shall provide 99.9% accuracy in knowledge tracing predictions so personalization is effective
Acceptance Criteria: AUC >= 0.85 for performance prediction; validated against held-out test sets; model drift monitoring; retraining pipeline
Notes/Evidence Base: Evidence: Modern DKT variants achieve AUC 0.85-0.89 (Mai et al. 2025)

User Story: System shall complete adaptive content recommendations within 200ms so the experience feels instantaneous
Acceptance Criteria: End-to-end latency from interaction to next content delivery <200ms at 95th percentile; includes model inference, database queries, and rendering
Notes/Evidence Base: Cognitive load management—delays disrupt flow

User Story: System shall implement differential privacy for analytics so aggregate data can be shared without individual exposure
Acceptance Criteria: Privacy budget (epsilon) configurable per query; noise injection for aggregate reports; certified differential privacy implementation
Notes/Evidence Base: Emerging best practice; not required for MVP
